response.justification: Sarah is a technically versatile engineer with a strong 15-year track record across backend, data, and infrastructure, and her current role at re:cinq shows genuine recent exposure to MCP tooling and agentic workflows. However, the role requires 8+ years of AI-driven solution development, and her GenAI work is limited to roughly one year at her current position. Python — the dominant language for GenAI engineering — is entirely absent from her skills. There is no mention of vector databases, RAG architectures, or LLM evaluation and optimization, which are explicit requirements. The gap between her current profile and the staff-level GenAI bar is too wide to close with interview performance alone.
strengths: Hands-on MCP (Model Context Protocol) server and agentic workflow infrastructure work at re:cinq directly maps to a listed key technology
Proven staff-level engineering leadership: organized code katas, delivered conference talks, created multi-day technical workshops
Demonstrated high-throughput data integration experience at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic (millions of requests/second, low-latency async systems)
concerns: Python is entirely absent from the skills list, disqualifying for a role where it is the primary GenAI development language
No mention of vector databases, RAG architectures, or LLM performance metrics — all explicit required or preferred qualifications
Substantive AI/GenAI experience is limited to approximately one year (April 2025–present), against an 8-year minimum requirement

key_factors.factor: Missing Python and GenAI stack (vector DBs, RAG, LLM optimization)
